metrics.label: Website Sessions
metrics.note: Up from 56 in October. Direct traffic (71) is the main driver.

metrics.label: New Users
metrics.note: More new users than active users — discovery is happening.

findings.title: Website Traffic Recovering (56 → 88)
findings.description: Back above the September baseline. Most visitors (71) are coming directly — consistent with referral-based discovery. Google organic (10) is growing, which means SEO groundwork is starting to pay off.

findings.title: Blog Infrastructure Opens a New Growth Channel
findings.description: The blog index page, destinations index, and Banff content create a foundation for SEO-driven traffic. Each new blog post is a new entry point for Google searchers — this compounds over time.

findings.title: Content Volume Remains the Core Challenge
findings.description: Only 2 LinkedIn posts published in all of Q4. The engagement is there when we post, but inconsistency means we can't build momentum or properly test LinkedIn as a channel.

actions.title: Blog Index Page Created
actions.description: New blog section on the website ready for ongoing content.

actions.title: Banff Corporate Travel Blog Published
actions.description: First destination-focused blog post live on the site.

actions.title: Destinations Index Page Created
actions.description: New destinations section for showcasing travel offerings.

actions.title: Banff Destination Page Live
actions.description: Dedicated landing page for Banff corporate travel.

actions.title: Site Architecture Improvements
actions.description: Structural updates to support blog and destinations content.

nextSteps: Jump on a call to discuss two potential paths forward for Q1 — both designed to solve the content bottleneck.
Explore ways to simplify content workflow so we can hit consistent posting targets.
Continue building out blog content to grow organic search traffic.
